headline: Edwin — Agentic AI Engineer
subheadline: I build production AI systems: multi-agent orchestration, MCP, RAG, voice and autonomous agents. Scroll to travel through the brain behind them.

projects.title: CorpChat
projects.tagline: Multi-tenant agentic SaaS where every company runs its own AI agent over MCP.
highlights: Retell AI voice agent + Cloud Run deploy
projects.problem: Businesses needed one AI agent that could chat with customers, book meetings, send email and capture leads across each company's own tools — securely and isolated per tenant.
architecture: Google ADK orchestrator with per-tenant subagents (gmail, calendar, zoom, hubspot, scraper)
7 MCP stdio servers expose external tools to the agent (Model Context Protocol)
Retell AI voice agent calls the backend over webhooks for phone conversations
Supabase Postgres with Row-Level Security; Fernet-encrypted OAuth tokens
Deployed on Google Cloud Run (australia-southeast1)
projects.role: Designed and built the full multi-tenant agent backend, the MCP servers, the voice integration and the security model.
proof: Per-tenant isolation with RLS + Fernet token encryption
Model-per-role cost control (flash for tools, pro for orchestration)

projects.title: Hermes × OpenClaw
projects.tagline: A brain/body multi-agent lab: reasoning and execution split over an atomic file bus.
highlights: Brain/Body split over an atomic file bus
projects.problem: Explore a resilient multi-agent design where a fast 'body' agent handles I/O and a deep 'brain' agent handles reasoning and persistent memory — without coupling them in memory.
architecture: OpenClaw (body) routes channels and dispatches mechanical tasks
Hermes (brain) holds SQLite long-term memory, self-improving skills and an audit log
Async communication via an atomic file bus (inbox -> processing -> done)
Telegram channel backend with a strict chat-id security gate
85+ tests including an end-to-end run with a real Gemini call
projects.role: Architected the brain/body split, the atomic handoff protocol and the conflict-resolution model (single source of truth per domain).
proof: Documented resolution of 14 potential cross-agent conflicts

projects.title: Study Hub
projects.tagline: Live class audio captured on-GPU and turned into structured knowledge and tasks.
highlights: One session_id for audio / YouTube / PDF
projects.problem: Turn hours of live lectures (Zoom/YouTube) into structured, searchable knowledge and auto-extract research tasks — fully local and private.
architecture: WASAPI loopback capture -> faster-whisper large-v3 on GPU (RTX 4070 Ti Super)
Gemini 2.5 Pro structures transcripts into typed notes (schema mode)
A single session_id contract unifies audio, YouTube and PDF ingestion
React SPA + FastAPI + WebSocket live transcript; Streamlit fallback
Auto-extracted tasks executed by a pluggable skills system
projects.role: Built the full real-time audio pipeline, the structuring agent and the skills / task-extraction system.
proof: One session_id contract for three input types

projects.title: LinkedIn Agent
projects.tagline: Autonomous LinkedIn agent with human-in-the-loop via Telegram.
projects.problem: Automate a LinkedIn presence (posts, job hunting, networking) without ever losing human control or risking the account.
architecture: Hexagonal architecture: core (portable skills) / adapters / runner
Skill contract run(task, ctx) -> dict, identical across projects (migration-ready)
Gemini drafts; approvals via Telegram inline keyboards; APScheduler cron
Secure TLS via the OS trust store (truststore), never verify=False
67/67 tests green
projects.role: Designed the 3-layer portable architecture and the strict human-in-the-loop approval flow.
proof: Zero auto-publish — every action gated by Telegram
Portable skill contract reused across projects

projects.title: A2A Scheduling
projects.tagline: A host agent orchestrates agents built in different frameworks over Google's A2A protocol.
projects.problem: Show that agents written in different frameworks (ADK, LangGraph, CrewAI) can interoperate and negotiate using the Agent-to-Agent protocol.
architecture: Host agent (ADK) coordinates the conversation
Friend agents implemented in ADK, LangGraph and CrewAI
Communication over the open A2A SDK protocol
Each agent is a self-contained service with its own dependencies
projects.role: Implemented the host orchestration and the cross-framework friend agents.
proof: Three frameworks interoperating via one protocol

projects.title: AI Capabilities Lab
projects.tagline: A multimodal sandbox: RAG, voice, TTS and live translation.
projects.problem: Build hands-on fluency across the full multimodal stack recruiters ask for in 2026.
architecture: RAG agent on Vertex AI with grounded retrieval
ADK voice assistant wired to Google Calendar
Dia text-to-speech model for audio generation
Real-time speech translation pipeline
projects.role: Built and integrated each capability as a standalone, composable demo.
proof: Built on Google ADK + Vertex AI

projects.title: Vibesclean
projects.tagline: Production marketing site + QR-driven booking funnel on Firebase.
projects.problem: A real business needed a fast marketing site with a frictionless booking funnel and reliable lead capture.
architecture: React + TypeScript + Vite, Tailwind v4
5-step booking state machine writing to Firestore (locked-down rules)
Fire-and-forget email notification with a 15s timeout fallback to WhatsApp
Framer Motion + GSAP animations, HLS video hero
Firebase Hosting (SPA)
projects.role: Built the entire site, the booking funnel and the Firebase deployment.
proof: Resilient booking with timeout + WhatsApp fallback

projects.title: Welby
projects.tagline: A live commercial web product: React front-end + Python backend.
projects.problem: Ship and operate a live commercial web product end to end.
architecture: React front-end
Python backend (API)
Deployed and serving real users at welby.com.au
projects.role: Contributed across front-end and backend for the live product.

projects.title: Chanblock
projects.tagline: On-chain mempool intelligence across Bitcoin & Ethereum to time investments.
projects.problem: Read each chain's mempool to surface pending / future transactions and infer where capital was about to move, to inform investment timing.
architecture: Bitcoin and Ethereum chain + mempool analysis
Pending-transaction inspection to anticipate near-future flows
Signal generation for investment timing
projects.role: Prototyped the mempool analysis and the signal logic.
proof: Archived prototype — described from memory, not re-analyzed
